title: EPG Endpoint Learning and Deletion Churn
exclusions: Pure **Nexus** **NX-OS** standalone fabrics without **APIC**; **MAC**-separation designs that intentionally oscillate **EPG** membership by automation (**IaC**) where churn KPIs are owned elsewhere; **acllog** five-tuple forensics as the only endpoint signal (**UC-18.3.6**); long-horizon **L3Out** prefix flap studies without **EPG** context (**UC-18.1.14**).
dataSources: `index=cisco_aci` (rename per governance) blending **five** sourcetypes: **`sourcetype=cisco:aci:endpoint`** (normalized **learn**, **delete**, **move**, **create**, **remove** verbs on **MAC**/**IP** rows tied to **`fvAEPg`**); **`sourcetype=cisco:aci:epm`** (**REST** snapshots of **`epmMacEp`**, **`epmIpEp`**, **`epmDb`** with **`dn`**, **`lc`**, **`encap`**); **`sourcetype=cisco:aci:fault`** (**faultInst** for **epm**/policy programming faults); **`sourcetype=cisco:aci:syslog`** (**APIC**/leaf mnemonics referencing **epm** learning); **`sourcetype=cisco:aci:audit`** (**aaaModLR** edits on **fvAEPg**, **fvRsPathAtt**, **l3extInst**). Primary **MO** poll: **`GET https://<apic>/api/class/epmMacEp.json`** (add **`epmIpEp`**/**`epmDb`** stanzas for parity) on **120–300s** cadence.
app: **Splunk_TA_cisco-aci** ([Splunkbase **4022**](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/4022)) for **`cisco:aci:syslog`**, **`cisco:aci:audit`**, and **`cisco:aci:endpoint`** parsers; **Splunk REST API Modular Input** ([Splunkbase **1546**](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/1546)) for **`/api/class/epmMacEp.json`**, **`/api/class/epmIpEp.json`**, and **`/api/class/epmDb.json`**; **Cisco Data Center Networking for Splunk Platform** ([Splunkbase **7777**](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/7777)) for starter **EPG** views; optional **Splunk_TA_vmware-nsxt** ([Splunkbase **4856**](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/4856)) and **Splunk_TA_vmware** ([Splunkbase **1810**](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/1810)) when **vMotion** calendars explain **MAC** motion.
spl:
index=cisco_aci sourcetype="cisco:aci:endpoint" earliest=-24h@m latest=now
| eval action_norm=lower(trim(coalesce(action,epm_action,change_type,"")))
| rex field=action_norm mode=sed "s/ing$//"
| eval action_bucket=case(match(action_norm,"learn|creat|add"),"learnish",match(action_norm,"delet|remov|mov|withdraw|clear"),"delish",true(),"other")
| eval mac_norm=upper(replace(trim(coalesce(mac,macAddr,macAddress,epm_mac,"")),"[.:-]",""))
| eval ip_norm=trim(coalesce(ip,ipAddr,ip_address,ipV4Addr,""))
| eval tenant=lower(trim(coalesce(tenant,tnName,fvTenant,tn,"unknown_tn")))
| eval epg=lower(trim(coalesce(epg,epgName,fvAEPg,aepg,"unknown_epg")))
| eval bd=lower(trim(coalesce(bd,bridgeDomain,fvBD,"unknown_bd")))
| where action_bucket IN ("learnish","delish")
| bin _time span=10m
| stats count as ep_events dc(mac_norm) as mac_moves dc(ip_norm) as ip_moves dc(action_bucket) as action_palette sum(eval(if(action_bucket="learnish",1,0))) as learnish_hits sum(eval(if(action_bucket="delish",1,0))) as delish_hits by _time tenant epg bd
| eventstats median(ep_events) as med_e median(mac_moves) as med_m by tenant
| eval churn_raw=ep_events+(1.5*mac_moves)
| eval ratio_e=if(med_e>0,ep_events/med_e,null())
| eval ratio_m=if(med_m>0,mac_moves/med_m,null())
| where (ep_events>=180 AND ratio_e>=5) OR (mac_moves>=50 AND ratio_m>=4) OR churn_raw>=max(350,6*(med_e+med_m+1))
| sort - churn_raw
| eval join_key="aci_epg_epm_churn_v1"
| join max=1 type=left join_key [
    search index=cisco_aci sourcetype="cisco:aci:epm" earliest=-24h@m latest=now
| eval mo=lower(trim(coalesce(classId,class,managedObjectClass,"")))
| eval lc_epm=lower(trim(coalesce(lc,lifecycle,"")))
| where match(mo,"epmmacep|epmipep|epmdb")
| stats count as epm_events dc(mo) as epm_mo_families dc(lc_epm) as lc_states
| eval join_key="aci_epg_epm_churn_v1"
    | eval epm_lane_ok=1
  ]
| join max=1 type=left join_key [
    search index=cisco_aci sourcetype="cisco:aci:fault" earliest=-24h@m latest=now
| eval lc_f=lower(trim(coalesce(lc,lifecycleState,lifecycle,"")))
| eval sev_f=lower(trim(coalesce(severity,fault_severity,code,"")))
| eval is_open=if(sev_f="cleared" OR lc_f IN ("cleared","resolved"),0,1)
| where is_open=1 AND (match(_raw,"epm|endpoint|lear|delet|routing") OR match(sev_f,"major|critical|warning"))
| stats count as open_ep_faults dc(sev_f) as sev_spread
| eval join_key="aci_epg_epm_churn_v1"
    | eval fault_lane_ok=1
  ]
| join max=1 type=left join_key [
    search index=cisco_aci sourcetype="cisco:aci:syslog" earliest=-24h@m latest=now
| eval txt=lower(_raw)
| where match(txt,"epm|endpoint learn|endpoint delete|maclearn|move|duplicate")
| stats count as syslog_ep_lines
| eval join_key="aci_epg_epm_churn_v1"
    | eval syslog_lane_ok=1
  ]
| join max=1 type=left join_key [
    search index=cisco_aci sourcetype="cisco:aci:audit" earliest=-24h@m latest=now
| eval mod=lower(trim(coalesce(ind,changingObj,aaamodlr_obj,"")))
| where match(mod,"fvaepg|fvbd|fvctx|l3ext|fvrsdom")
| stats count as audit_epg_ops dc(mod) as touched_mos
| eval join_key="aci_epg_epm_churn_v1"
    | eval audit_lane_ok=1
  ]
| table _time tenant epg bd churn_raw ep_events mac_moves learnish_hits delish_hits med_e med_m ratio_e ratio_m action_palette ip_moves epm_lane_ok epm_events epm_mo_families lc_states fault_lane_ok open_ep_faults sev_spread syslog_lane_ok syslog_ep_lines audit_lane_ok audit_epg_ops touched_mos
description: Detects abnormal **EPG**-scoped **endpoint** learning and deletion churn on **Cisco ACI** by rolling up **`cisco:aci:endpoint`** learnish versus delish bursts per **tenant**, **EPG**, and **bridge domain**, then proving feed health with **`cisco:aci:epm`**, **`cisco:aci:fault`**, **`cisco:aci:syslog`**, and **`cisco:aci:audit`** joins so **NetOps** can separate **duplicate IP** storms, **VM mobility** noise, **policy** edits, and **epm** programming faults before **east-west** paths wobble.
value: Application owners keep **SLAs** intact because **database** and **storage** east-west flows stop flapping silently when **control-plane** **epm** work is red-flagged beside **change** records. **Capacity** planners see which **tenants** stress **leaf** **endpoint** databases, **finance**-adjacent **tiers** get defensible **bridge** narratives, and **incident** timelines shorten when **MAC/IP** churn ties to **fault** codes instead of hours of **pcap** guesswork.
implementation: Land **Splunkbase** **4022** lanes for **endpoint** trackers plus **1546** HTTPS pulls for **`epmMacEp`**, **`epmIpEp`**, and **`epmDb`**, normalize **FIELDALIAS** for **faultInst** and **aaaModLR**, schedule **aci_uc18121_epg_epm_churn_core** every **10m**, maintain **aci_epg_allowlist.csv** for approved migration **EPGs**, and pair with **7777** macros after index rename.
visualization: **Timechart** of **learnish_hits** versus **delish_hits** per **EPG**; **single-value** **churn_raw** with **24h** sparkline; **table** of **tenant**, **epg**, **bd**, **mac_moves**, **ratio_e**, **lane** flags; **column** chart of **open_ep_faults** stacked by **severity**.
schema: **Inventory** alignment treats stable **MAC**/**IP** tuples as endpoint objects when **dn** paths include **`fvAEPg`**; **Change** alignment tags **audit** rows that touch **fvAEPg**/**fvBD** before churn windows; **Network_Traffic** alignment is indirect—pair with **acllog** feeds when five-tuple proof is required.

references.title: Cisco APIC Management Information Model — epmMacEp
Cisco APIC Management Information Model — epmIpEp
Cisco APIC Management Information Model — epmDb
Cisco APIC REST API Class Query — epmMacEp.json
Cisco Data Center Networking for Splunk (Splunkbase 7777)

detailedImplementation:
**Prerequisites**
• **APIC** clusters must expose HTTPS 443/tcp to collectors with read-only RBAC for **`epmMacEp`**, **`epmIpEp`**, **`epmDb`**, **`faultInst`**, and **`aaaModLR`** without configuration write paths that could destabilize production tenants.
• Install **Splunk_TA_cisco-aci** ([Splunkbase **4022**](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/4022)) on heavy or universal forwarders fronting syslog so **`cisco:aci:syslog`** and **`cisco:aci:audit`** stay on separate props lanes—parser rollbacks stay surgical when Cisco ships field renames.
• Deploy **Splunk REST API Modular Input** ([Splunkbase **1546**](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/1546)) with **POST /api/aaaLogin.json** plus rotating **POST /api/aaaRefresh.json** sessions, then schedule **GET /api/class/epmMacEp.json?rsp-subtree=no** on 120–300s cadence; add sibling stanzas for /api/class/epmIpEp.json and /api/class/epmDb.json when IP motion or database pressure must be proven—not only MAC churn.
• Normalize **`cisco:aci:endpoint`** streams so **learnish** verbs map from learn, learning, created, or added tokens while **delish** verbs map from delete, removed, move, or withdraw variants your **TA** version emits.
• Keep **`cisco:aci:epm`** MO class strings (**epmMacEp**, **epmIpEp**, **epmDb**) lowercase in eval guards so join health flags do not oscillate on case drift.
• Optional VMware context: **Splunk_TA_vmware-nsxt** ([Splunkbase **4856**](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/4856)) and **Splunk_TA_vmware** ([Splunkbase **1810**](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/1810)) correlate vMotion windows with **EPG** churn so **NOC** pages cite hypervisor calendars—not fabric defects alone.
• **Index** sizing: busy multi-tenant fabrics can emit 50k–400k epm-adjacent events daily—retain 15–30 days hot for replay after TAC investigations.
• **Visore** literacy: operators must know how fvAEPg, fvBD, and fvCtx DN fragments appear inside epmMacEp dn paths so Splunk drilldowns match APIC GUI filters.
• **Microsegmentation** note: **vzAny** attachments widen learning visibility; tune thresholds when security teams intentionally flood learn events during quarantine moves.

**Telemetry discipline** — Endpoint churn is a symptom bundle: rapid learn or delete pairs strain leaf epm databases, inflate COOP gossip, and often precede faultInst codes referencing programming pressure. Pair **`cisco:aci:endpoint`** rollups with **`cisco:aci:epm`** MO snapshots so null join flags mean blind analytics—not a calm cloth. Syslog lines that mention MAC learning without structured MO rows should not bypass ratio gates. Audit bursts on fvAEPg during churn windows usually mean operator intent or automation replay—not silent hardware failure. Vocabulary anchors: encap, lc, rn, and debugMACAddr fields differ by export path; l3ext objects matter when east-west churn is really north-south route flap bleed-through. Starter dashboards on **Splunkbase** **7777** help only after macros match your **index=cisco_aci** governance names.

**Step 1** — **Configure data collection**
• **REST** stanza A polls https://<apic>/api/class/epmMacEp.json with rsp-subtree=no for steady endpoint MAC rows; include order-by=epmMacEp.modTs when incremental strategies are supported so poll volume stays bounded.
• REST stanza B targets /api/class/epmIpEp.json for duplicate IP investigations where MAC counts look quiet yet control-plane work remains hot.
• REST stanza C samples /api/class/epmDb.json when Cisco TAC asks for database pressure corroboration alongside Splunk math.
• Map exports into **`cisco:aci:epm`** with **FIELDALIAS** symmetry on dn, lc, classId, and modTs so join filters behave identically across apic nodes.
• Enable APIC remote syslog for mnemonics that cite endpoint learning, duplicate detection, or epm faults; keep **LINE_BREAKER** tuned for multi-line fault narratives.
• Stream **faultInst** into **`cisco:aci:fault`** with cleared lifecycle parity between REST and syslog transports.
• Mirror **aaaModLR** into **`cisco:aci:audit`** with ind preserved for fvAEPg or fvBD objects tied to churny tenants.
• Document collector hostnames beside APIC VIP CMDB rows—DNS drift makes join lanes read zero while events still flow to Splunk.

**Step 2** — **Create the search**
Paste the **spl** field into Search; save as **aci_uc18121_epg_epm_churn_core** after renaming **index=cisco_aci** if governance mandates **dc_network** or macro wrappers.

```spl
index=cisco_aci sourcetype="cisco:aci:endpoint" earliest=-24h@m latest=now
| eval action_norm=lower(trim(coalesce(action,epm_action,change_type,"")))
| rex field=action_norm mode=sed "s/ing$//"
| eval action_bucket=case(match(action_norm,"learn|creat|add"),"learnish",match(action_norm,"delet|remov|mov|withdraw|clear"),"delish",true(),"other")
| eval mac_norm=upper(replace(trim(coalesce(mac,macAddr,macAddress,epm_mac,"")),"[.:-]",""))
| eval ip_norm=trim(coalesce(ip,ipAddr,ip_address,ipV4Addr,""))
| eval tenant=lower(trim(coalesce(tenant,tnName,fvTenant,tn,"unknown_tn")))
| eval epg=lower(trim(coalesce(epg,epgName,fvAEPg,aepg,"unknown_epg")))
| eval bd=lower(trim(coalesce(bd,bridgeDomain,fvBD,"unknown_bd")))
| where action_bucket IN ("learnish","delish")
| bin _time span=10m
| stats count as ep_events dc(mac_norm) as mac_moves dc(ip_norm) as ip_moves dc(action_bucket) as action_palette sum(eval(if(action_bucket="learnish",1,0))) as learnish_hits sum(eval(if(action_bucket="delish",1,0))) as delish_hits by _time tenant epg bd
| eventstats median(ep_events) as med_e median(mac_moves) as med_m by tenant
| eval churn_raw=ep_events+(1.5*mac_moves)
| eval ratio_e=if(med_e>0,ep_events/med_e,null())
| eval ratio_m=if(med_m>0,mac_moves/med_m,null())
| where (ep_events>=180 AND ratio_e>=5) OR (mac_moves>=50 AND ratio_m>=4) OR churn_raw>=max(350,6*(med_e+med_m+1))
| sort - churn_raw
| eval join_key="aci_epg_epm_churn_v1"
| join max=1 type=left join_key [ search index=cisco_aci sourcetype="cisco:aci:epm" earliest=-24h@m latest=now | eval mo=lower(trim(coalesce(classId,class,managedObjectClass,""))) | eval lc_epm=lower(trim(coalesce(lc,lifecycle,""))) | where match(mo,"epmmacep|epmipep|epmdb") | stats count as epm_events dc(mo) as epm_mo_families dc(lc_epm) as lc_states | eval join_key="aci_epg_epm_churn_v1" | eval epm_lane_ok=1 ]
| join max=1 type=left join_key [ search index=cisco_aci sourcetype="cisco:aci:fault" earliest=-24h@m latest=now | eval lc_f=lower(trim(coalesce(lc,lifecycleState,lifecycle,""))) | eval sev_f=lower(trim(coalesce(severity,fault_severity,code,""))) | eval is_open=if(sev_f="cleared" OR lc_f IN ("cleared","resolved"),0,1) | where is_open=1 AND (match(_raw,"epm|endpoint|lear|delet|routing") OR match(sev_f,"major|critical|warning")) | stats count as open_ep_faults dc(sev_f) as sev_spread | eval join_key="aci_epg_epm_churn_v1" | eval fault_lane_ok=1 ]
| join max=1 type=left join_key [ search index=cisco_aci sourcetype="cisco:aci:syslog" earliest=-24h@m latest=now | eval txt=lower(_raw) | where match(txt,"epm|endpoint learn|endpoint delete|maclearn|move|duplicate") | stats count as syslog_ep_lines | eval join_key="aci_epg_epm_churn_v1" | eval syslog_lane_ok=1 ]
| join max=1 type=left join_key [ search index=cisco_aci sourcetype="cisco:aci:audit" earliest=-24h@m latest=now | eval mod=lower(trim(coalesce(ind,changingObj,aaamodlr_obj,""))) | where match(mod,"fvaepg|fvbd|fvctx|l3ext|fvrsdom") | stats count as audit_epg_ops dc(mod) as touched_mos | eval join_key="aci_epg_epm_churn_v1" | eval audit_lane_ok=1 ]
| table _time tenant epg bd churn_raw ep_events mac_moves learnish_hits delish_hits med_e med_m ratio_e ratio_m action_palette ip_moves epm_lane_ok epm_events epm_mo_families lc_states fault_lane_ok open_ep_faults sev_spread syslog_lane_ok syslog_ep_lines audit_lane_ok audit_epg_ops touched_mos
```

**Understanding this SPL**
• **Scope** — **`cisco:aci:endpoint`** anchors the math on verbs that track EPG attachment changes rather than guessing solely from syslog prose.
• **action_bucket** — Collapses vendor tokens into learnish versus delish families so threshold tuning stays stable across TA releases.
• **mac_norm** — Strips punctuation so the same NIC does not look like four hosts during move storms.
• Time bucketing — span=10m balances REST latency with enough samples; widen to 15m on small fabrics to dampen median noise.
• **eventstats** — med_e and med_m build per-tenant baselines so lab tenants do not page on dozens of events while prod still bleeps.
• **Thresholds** — ratio_e captures event bursts; ratio_m captures distinct MAC instability; mac_moves gates catch micro-flap patterns when totals look modest.
• **Join** lanes — epm_lane_ok, fault_lane_ok, syslog_lane_ok, and audit_lane_ok prove five sourcetypes produced bytes in-window—zeroes mean incomplete telemetry.
• Outputs — action_palette shows whether churn is symmetric learn or delete or biased—guiding TAC next steps.

**Step 3** — **Validate**
• Compare **churn_raw** peaks to APIC Operations → Endpoints views filtered on the same tenant and EPG—accept ±1 bucket skew when REST lags syslog one poll.
• Spot-check three **epmMacEp** dn samples per incident in Visore to confirm leaf attachment matches Splunk extractions.
• Run ****metadata type=sources** on **cisco_aci** for the day—verify **`cisco:aci:endpoint`**, **`cisco:aci:epm`**, **`cisco:aci:fault`**, **`cisco:aci:syslog`**, and **`cisco:aci:audit`** all show recentTime unless strategically disabled.
• Cross-read open_ep_faults with APIC Faults UI focusing on programming families tied to policy or epm pressure.
• During changes, capture notes in **aci_epg_allowlist.csv** so reviewers know migration windows authorized the spike.

**Step 4** — **Operationalize**
• Publish a **dashboard** with role-based read for NOC and write for automation owners; embed drilldown tokens tenant, epg, and _time linking to stored Visore deep links.
• Schedule aci_uc18121_epg_epm_churn_core every 10m with summary indexing via collect when executives need 30d trends.
• Wire aci_epg_allowlist.csv on _time to auto-tag maintenance, VMware motions, and LLPG rebuilds.
• Export weekly CSV snapshots (tenant, churn_raw, mac_moves) to problem management so noisy EPGs stay visible.
• Mirror alerts into **ITSM** tickets with deeplinks to **`cisco:aci:audit`** editors touching the same EPG DN.

**Step 5** — **Operationalize & Troubleshoot**
**Visualization** — Top row: **single-value** churn_raw on the last bucket plus distinct EPG count with sparkline **timechart** span=15m on learnish_hits. Middle: column chart stacking learnish_hits versus delish_hits per epg with dotted baseline for yesterday p95. Bottom: sortable table on churn_raw showing med_e, ratio_e, open_ep_faults, audit_epg_ops, syslog_ep_lines, epm_events, and lane OK flags colorized red when zero while ep_events stayed hot.

**Alert** design — Trigger when ratio_e>=5 and ep_events>=180 for two consecutive 10m buckets OR mac_moves>=50 with ratio_m>=4 once fault_lane_ok=1. Throttle 45m per tenant and epg. Include top three MAC prefixes, sev_spread, touched_mos, and links to change tickets. Recovery notice when churn_raw falls below 1.5 times the tenant median for six buckets.

**Failure** modes — Null tenant extractions imply blank dn—fix TA **FIELDALIAS** stanzas. Join zeroes with flowing endpoint lanes imply index ACL or sourcetype typos. HTTP 401 or 503 pair with aaaRefresh staleness or APIC leader moves—use **7777** health panels for **Raft** context.

Starter panels from Splunkbase 7777 stay credible only after macros match this index layout—misaligned macros create false emptiness—not fabric health.
grandmaExplanation: We track when the fabric keeps learning and forgetting the same computers in a frantic loop, so teams can spot duplicate addresses or shaky moves before everyday apps go quiet.
hardware: Cisco APIC clusters and Cisco Nexus 9000 switches in ACI mode
knownFalsePositives:
**vmotion_mac_shuffle** — VMware vMotion legitimately replays MAC learning across leaf switches during maintenance. Cross-check `cisco:aci:audit` for quiet policy windows and `vmware:vsphere:task` when that index exists before paging fabric owners.

**epm_poll_drift** — REST collectors that miss POST /api/aaaRefresh.json cadence duplicate epmMacEp rows across adjacent polls, inflating ep_events without new leaf work. Dedup on _time, dn, and a hashed _raw fingerprint, or widen bins until HTTP 429 storms settle.

**parser_field_rename** — TA upgrades that rename action to epm_action can starve the learnish bucket until FIELDALIAS returns. Watch for action_palette stuck on other while syslog still mentions learning—patch aliases before retuning thresholds.

**syslog_regex_echo** — Stale syslog lines that mention endpoint moves during unrelated control-plane advisories can lift syslog_ep_lines while cisco:aci:endpoint stays calm. Require ratio_e corroboration from structured feeds.

**duplicate_ip_clusters** — Misconfigured servers issuing duplicate IPs force rapid delete or learn cycles that look like automation bugs. Validate ARP tables and IPAM assignments before blaming the fabric.

**faultinst_delegate_stack** — Parent and child faultInst rows for the same EPG can double open_ep_faults unless delegated fault SOPs filter duplicates—keep lifecycle parity across syslog and REST transports.

**microseg_quarantine_sweep** — Security tools that repeatedly shunt hosts between quarantine and production EPGs create intentional churn. Route those EPGs through **`aci_epg_allowlist.csv`** or a dedicated correlation saved search so pages stay meaningful.

**issu_epm_resync_wave** — ISSU and controller-image steps occasionally replay `epmDb` synchronization, which concentrates learnish spikes for a bounded maintenance window. Require two negative buckets after the change record closes or gate alerts on `epm_lane_ok` so authorized maintenance is not treated as an unknown storm.

**coop_partition_chatter** — Rare COOP healing periods can push move-like syslog narratives while structured MO exports already stabilize. Compare syslog_ep_lines against ratio_e from `cisco:aci:endpoint` and validate APIC cluster health from **UC-18.1.1** before swapping leaf hardware.
